content.text:
**Trails read path is implemented and TO COMMIT.** Summary:

- `/__issues`, `/__issue`, `/__issues/search` now source from `entire trail list --json`, mapped to the issue shape the Issues tab already binds (`trail_to_issue`: `number`, `title`, `body`, `status`→`state`, `author.login`, `created_at`→`createdAt`, `labels`, `url`). No XMLUI change needed.
- **Verified**: clean build (no warnings), `trail_issue_tests` pass, and the mapping checked against real `entirehq/entire.io` trails. Cleaned up the schema-probe trail I'd created (bram trails back to `[]`).

Two things you should know before committing:

1. **I delivered only the read path.** The **write path stays on `gh`** for now — `/__issue/comment` and `/__issue/close`. Reason: trails have code **findings** + a status lifecycle, *not* issue-style comments, so comment→? is a real design decision; and close is entangled with the GitHub close-on-commit machinery that the UI phase must handle as a unit. Close alone maps cleanly (`entire trail update --status closed`), but shouldn't land piecemeal. So this isn't fully "no more GitHub" yet — the read path is done, writes are a follow-up. Recorded in the draft.

2. **After relaunch, the Issues tab will be empty** — `gh/evjan/bram` has zero trails. That's expected (fresh trails repo), not a bug; it'll populate as trails get created. The tab's still labeled "Issues" (UI rename → Trails is a later phase).

It's TO COMMIT — approve when ready (I'll commit with `--no-verify` since the `prepare-commit-msg` hook still hangs). Want me to **propose the write-path follow-up item** (comment/close → trail findings + status) so it's tracked?

Worth a Phase-2 refinement: derive the session label from the first user prompt (via --transcript/session info) rather than the earliest checkpoint's message, so titles stay stable across condensation. Want me to propose that as a worklist item?
Read and follow this Bram turn: @resources/outbound-turns/1783202716441-turn.json @/Users/peterevjan/.cache/bram/paste/1783202701578-327fa919.png (end of Bram turn)

content.text: Found them: the nav label is `Main.xmlui:335` (`<NavLink label="Issues" to="/issues" />`) and the rest — heading, "+ New issue", "opened", "comments", search placeholder, empty state — are user-facing strings in `Issues.xmlui`. That's the UI-rename phase. Proposing it:

input.content:
# Before

The Issues tab now reads from Entire trails (committed read path), but
every user-facing label still says "Issues" / "issue". Confirmed live:
the tab renders a real trail ("#2 Test trail", *evjan opened 1 minute
ago*) under the heading **Issues**, with a **+ New issue** button, an
**Issues** nav item, and a search placeholder reading "Search title,
body, comments, author…". So the data is trails but the vocabulary is
GitHub issues.

Label locations:

- `app/tools/Main.xmlui:335` — `<NavLink label="Issues" to="/issues" />`
  (left-nav item).
- `app/tools/components/Issues.xmlui` — heading "Issues", "+ New issue"
  button, "opened" / "comments" wording, search placeholder, empty
  state, and the expanded-detail copy.

# After

Relabel the **user-facing** strings from "Issues"/"issue" to
"Trails"/"trail":

- `Main.xmlui`: NavLink `label="Issues"` → `label="Trails"`.
- `Issues.xmlui`: heading → "Trails"; "+ New issue" → "+ New trail";
  search placeholder → drop "comments" (trails have findings, not
  comments) e.g. "Search title, body, author…"; "opened" wording and
  empty-state text → trail phrasing; expanded-detail copy.

Deliberately **out of scope** (kept as-is to avoid churn and risk with
no user-visible payoff):

- Internal identifiers — component name `Issues`, file `Issues.xmlui`,
  route `/issues`, `var.newIssueText` etc., and the backend `/__issues*`
  route names. These aren't visible; renaming them is pure churn and
  would risk the working read path. Can be revisited later if desired.
- The "comments" concept itself. The read path returns `comments: []`;
  wiring trail **findings** into that surface is part of the deferred
  write-path item, not this cosmetic pass.

XMLUI-only, so it hot-reloads — no rebuild. No file overlap with the
`migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails` item (that's `lib.rs`), so the two
are independent.

content.text:
Proposed **`rename-issues-ui-to-trails`** (TO APPLY). It relabels the user-facing strings — nav item, heading, "+ New trail", search placeholder (dropping "comments"), "opened"/empty-state wording — from Issues→Trails across `Main.xmlui` and `Issues.xmlui`. XMLUI-only, so it hot-reloads (no rebuild).

Deliberately scoped to **visible labels only**: I'm leaving the internal names untouched (component `Issues`, `/issues` route, `/__issues*` backend routes, `var.newIssue*`). They're invisible, and renaming them is pure churn that'd risk the working read path — noted in the draft.

Where things stand in the Worklist:
- **`migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails`** — TO COMMIT (read path). Independent of the rename (different files), so you can approve/commit it whenever.
- **`rename-issues-ui-to-trails`** — TO APPLY (new).

content.text:
**Root cause found.** The `entire hooks git prepare-commit-msg` command **reads stdin and blocks until EOF**:
- stdin = `/dev/null` (immediate EOF) → returns instantly.
- stdin = an open pipe (never EOFs) → **hangs forever**.

That's exactly the difference between my standalone tests (pipe closed → fast) and a real commit: when Bram's worklist-commit route spawns `git commit`, the child inherits a stdin that never EOFs, so the hook's stdin read blocks and the whole commit wedges. The durable fix in *our* code is to spawn git with null stdin (Bram commits non-interactively; hooks shouldn't read the committer's stdin). Let me find where the route spawns the commit.

content.text:
**Root cause nailed.** It's a stdin bug, and it's in *our* code:

- `entire hooks git prepare-commit-msg` reads stdin and blocks until EOF (proven: `/dev/null` → instant, open pipe → hangs forever).
- Bram's commit route runs `git` via `git_run_owned` (`lib.rs:26617`), which uses `.output()` **without setting stdin**. So the spawned `git commit` — and its `prepare-commit-msg` hook — inherits Bram's stdin, which never EOFs. The hook's stdin read blocks → the commit wedges and holds `index.lock`.

The fix is one line: spawn git with `Stdio::null()` stdin (Bram commits non-interactively; neither git nor its hooks should read Bram's stdin). Let me propose it — and flag an ordering issue first.

input.content:
# Before

Committing through Bram (the `/__worklist/commit` route, and by extension
the Commits-tab commit) hangs indefinitely and leaves a `.git/index.lock`
behind. Every commit this session had to be forced through with
`git commit --no-verify`.

**Root cause (confirmed empirically):** Entire's `prepare-commit-msg`
git hook (`.git/hooks/prepare-commit-msg` → `entire hooks git
prepare-commit-msg`) **reads stdin and blocks until EOF**:

- stdin = `/dev/null` (immediate EOF) → returns instantly.
- stdin = an open pipe that never sends EOF → hangs forever.

Bram's shared git runner `git_run_owned` (`src-tauri/src/lib.rs:26617`)
spawns git with `std::process::Command::new("git")….output()` and never
sets `stdin`. `.output()` leaves the child inheriting Bram's stdin, which
(as a long-lived desktop process) never EOFs. So the `git commit` it runs
for the commit route — and the `prepare-commit-msg` hook git launches —
inherit that stdin, the hook's read blocks, and the commit wedges. Run
standalone from a shell (stdin closes → EOF) the hook returns instantly,
which is why it looked intermittent.

# After

In `git_run_owned`, spawn git with a null stdin:

```rust
std::process::Command::new("git")
    .current_dir(&root)
    .args(args)
    .stdin(std::process::Stdio::null())   // <- added
    .output()
```

Bram runs git non-interactively; git never needs Bram's stdin, and this
gives the hook an immediate EOF instead of a blocking read. `git_run_owned`
is used only by the commit route (git add + git commit), so the fix is
scoped to exactly the hang path.

Scope / notes:

- Read-only git spawns elsewhere (`git diff/log/status`) don't run hooks
  and don't read stdin, so they're unaffected; no change needed.
- `git push` (pre-push hook) already works and goes through a different
  path — out of scope here.
- **Upstream caveat:** a `prepare-commit-msg` hook that blocks on stdin is
  arguably an Entire CLI bug — a plain `git commit` typed in a terminal
  could still hang (tty stdin, no EOF until Ctrl-D). This Bram-side fix
  covers all *Bram-driven* commits; worth also reporting to Entire so the
  hook redirects its own stdin.

Entanglement: this touches `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`, which currently holds
the **uncommitted, approved** trails read-path change
(`migrate-github-gh-to-entire-trails`, TO COMMIT). Recommend committing
that item first (it's verified) so the two land as separate commits;
otherwise this fix mixes into its diff.

content.text:
Fix proposed as **`git-commit-null-stdin-hook-hang`** (TO APPLY) — the one-line `Stdio::null()` change to `git_run_owned`, with the root-cause writeup and evidence in the draft.

One ordering wrinkle to decide, because it's a real entanglement: the fix touches `lib.rs`, which right now holds the **uncommitted, approved** trails read-path change. If I apply the fix on top, the two mix into one diff. Cleanest sequence:

1. **Commit the trails read-path first** (it's approved + verified) — one more `--no-verify` commit, which clears `lib.rs`.
2. **Approve + apply the hook fix** — I add `Stdio::null()`, rebuild, relaunch.
3. **Commit the hook fix** (the *last* `--no-verify` commit).
4. From then on, commits through Bram's route no longer hang — no more `--no-verify`.

So: approve **`git-commit-null-stdin-hook-hang`**, and confirm you're OK with me committing the trails item first to keep the two commits clean. (Or if you'd rather I keep `lib.rs` untouched by trails and just fix the hook, say so and I'll sort the ordering differently.)

One honesty note in the draft: a hook that blocks on stdin is really an Entire-CLI bug — a plain `git commit` typed in a terminal could still hang. This Bram-side fix covers all Bram-driven commits; worth also flagging upstream to Entire so the hook redirects its own stdin.
